data.epi_content:
After losing my hand, I felt nothing at all—as if it had simply vanished.

When I sensed something was wrong and looked at my right hand, I realized it had fallen off.

Looking down, I saw my hand still gripping the pistol on the classroom floor, detached yet perfectly intact.

Strangely, no blood was seeping from the severed surface.

This wasn't a feeling of numbness from excitement or mental confusion.

It was more like my hand existed in a different space altogether.

While I was immersed in fascination, or perhaps slight fear, the Crown Prince spoke.

"Are you... the daughter of Duke Mecklenburg?"

I barely paid attention to whatever the blond man was saying with his bewildered face.

"Why doesn't it hurt? There's no blood either."

Feeling slightly dejected, I pulled out a chair with my left hand, sat down, and crossed my legs.

I closed my eyes once, and when I opened them again, I thought how nice it would be to smoke a cigarette.

Unfortunately, I had nothing.

"Why did you try to shoot me?"

"Because I saw you."

"You shot because you saw me? What a remarkable answer.

There shouldn't be a single person in this country who doesn't recognize my face."

I didn't actually see his face.

I might have glimpsed his appearance briefly, but not many people would assume someone is the Crown Prince just because they're tall with blond hair.

Though he did seem to have a certain glow about him.

"Your face, well. Let's just say I couldn't see it clearly from a distance."

At my words, the man's eyebrows twitched slightly as if annoyed.

Then he began spitting out harsh words.

"When your family collapses from disaster and you have no one left around you, why not just die quietly in your room instead of causing such a huge incident? Why did it have to happen while I was attending!"

He says he doesn't understand.

Well, this person could never live a miserable life even if he wanted to.

Not unless he experienced all kinds of absurd misfortunes like me.

It's natural he doesn't understand.

At first, I tried to die just as he suggested.

And I probably succeeded, but somehow failed for some reason.

For this person to end up like me, this country would have to collapse—I wonder if that's even possible.

There are dissidents, fanatics, all sorts of people, but this is a stable and prosperous nation.

The nobles couldn't rise up either.

The Emperor can force his sons and daughters to come to the Capital—nobles are nothing compared to that.

There's no reason for rebellion.

So I simply answered, remembering how I had given up and lay in the bathtub, dying in a slightly pathetic yet ridiculous way.

"I tried, but it didn't work, Your Highness."

After hearing my answer, he crossed his arms and stood still for quite some time.

What was that startled look when he first entered this classroom? Was he possessed by some ghost?

Now he's completely calm.

Like someone incapable of showing surprise.

He gave orders to someone in the empty air, then walked over to pick up my severed hand with the pistol still tightly gripped in it.

I could hear the popping sounds as he pried each finger loose, one by one.

"Should this be banned too? No. This probably happened because there's no one who knows how to properly handle a sword or magic."

He tossed the pistol aside and brought my severed hand to me.

When he pressed the severed surfaces together, my hand reattached seamlessly, like magnetic dolls connecting.

At the same time, sensation returned.
data.epi_content2:
It was incredibly strange.

When had anyone ever seen a severed arm reattach without any blood, without even seeing red?

Among the magic I knew, nothing like this existed—it must be some unique magic known only to the imperial family.

"What was your name again?"

"Erica Mecklenburg."

"Explain why you killed them."

"Would anything change if I told you?"

"I don't know."

Feeling slightly chilled, I looked around and saw Lydia with a hole in her head, along with other dead students lying in rows.

While I wasn't looking, unknown people must have come to arrange them.

Or perhaps this man before me had done something bizarre again.

"Why did you kill these students lying here?"

Four of them, I think.

A number that could be considered many or few, depending on perspective.

I shrugged my shoulders with a clueless expression.

Honestly, I thought I'd be dragged to some underground dungeon, but suddenly I'm having a private audience with the Crown Prince.

It's a bit confusing.

"So why did you do this?"

He's persistent.

Without even glancing at the corpses, he sits in a chair that appeared from nowhere, crosses his legs, and fidgets annoyingly.

"Answer me."

As I kept my mouth shut and stared at my reattached right hand, he pulled out a pipe from his breast pocket.

He put the antique-looking pipe in his mouth, filled it with leaves from a rectangular box, and created a tiny spark at his fingertip.

When he lit it, white smoke rose creating a haze.

It felt like incense being burned to honor departed souls.

The Crown Prince inhaled the smoke once, exhaled, then spoke again.

"I've reattached your hand, so how about repaying me with an answer?"

"...Ah."

Repayment, huh? This lady wouldn't stay quiet after hearing that.

Well, I should be grateful he hasn't killed me yet.

"Your Highness, though it's unlikely for you, imagine if this empire suddenly collapsed and someone like, say, the chancellor—

Someone you once controlled suddenly turned against you, trying to use and torment you.

To the point where life becomes difficult."

So I gave up and just tried to die.

Tried to escape through death.

"But the only escape route disappeared.

Someone blocked it, though I don't know who."

I took a breath and exhaled after saying this.

My head felt slightly dizzy.

"If the future holds not a glimmer of hope, and in that torment, everyone who loves you either leaves or dies.

If Your Highness were in such a situation, what choice would you make?"

It's somewhat impertinent, but not as impertinent as sitting cross-legged next to corpses.

"Would anything change if I told you?"

What a petty person.

Probably the type to hold grudges and get revenge later if he hears someone talking behind his back.

I bit my lip in momentary irritation.

A little blood seeped out.
data.epi_content3:
So, my slightly bitten lip bleeds this much, yet what happened to the blood from my severed wrist?

I don't know.

Even if I knew, I doubt it would lead to any revelation.

"Since nothing will change anyway, just think of it as rambling in front of corpses."

Hearing this, he let out a mocking laugh.

As if he quite liked my answer.

Future corpse, that is.

If they let me live after causing this incident, either this person or this country would have to be insane.

"A decent excuse."

He removed the pipe from his mouth for a moment.

"Well... if the chancellor ordered me to do something, I would kiss his feet if that's what he demanded.

And no matter how much I was tormented, I would endlessly pursue life. Even if the end was an ugly death."

His confident demeanor made me laugh mockingly.

But he continued undisturbed, speaking like a boy sharing his dreams, with what seemed like pure aspiration.

"I wouldn't care what kind of people surrounded me, or what people I lost.

If the empire collapsed, I would rebuild it with my own hands.

If a beloved lover died, burying them in my heart with some sadness would be enough.

If a former subordinate tried to control and humiliate me, I would hide a dagger in my heart while submitting, waiting for the right moment.

And at least, I wouldn't choose to end everything like this.

I'd rather hang myself alone in a room corner."

That's exactly what didn't work, which is why I'm doing this.

"So, what will you do now?"

"What are you asking?"

"The manner of your death, of course.

First option: dangling before a crowd, kicking your feet in the air, dancing until you die."

I probably wouldn't die easily even after being hanged.

They would slowly shorten the rope, allowing the angry crowd to throw stones or tomatoes.

The rope would likely be loosely tied.

They'd want to see me struggling for 30-40 minutes.

"Second option: kneeling, with an axe splitting your head in half."

Well, this might actually be better.

Though if they assign an incompetent executioner, I might live for hours.

"Or third: living as a specimen for magicians' live experiments until you die..."

Feeling like he might go on to the hundredth option, I interrupted the Crown Prince.

"Please choose the least painful one."

At my words, he mockingly replied.

"Four students died in here.

And usually when such tragedies occur, people point fingers at His Majesty the Emperor.

If you've committed something, it's a noble's duty to take responsibility. Isn't that right?"

"I wonder, should I bear obligations when I have nothing left?"

"Of course, thanks to this, it will be much easier to legally confiscate your territory.

There's no bloodline left in your family except for that grotesque half-wit."

He speaks without erasing his smile, as if enjoying himself.

"Anyway, you'll have to die in a somewhat horrific manner.

Just like your brother."
